title: Device and method for non-invasive assessment of maximum arterial compliance
abstract: The present invention relates to a device (10) for determining an arterial compliance of a subject (12). The device (10) comprises an inflatable cuff (14), a pressure sensor (18) which is configured to sense a pressure signal (52) that is indicative of a pressure within the cuff (14), a second sensor (20) which is at least partly integrated in the cuff (14) and configured to sense a second signal (56) that is responsive to expansions and contractions of the cuff (14) caused by a pulsating blood flow in an artery (50) of the subject (12), and a processing unit (22). The processing unit is configured to determine based on said part of the pressure signal (52) a pulse pressure of the subject (12), to determine based on said part of the second signal (56) an arterial volume change of the artery (50) of the subject (12) during at least one cardiac cycle, and to determine the arterial compliance of the subject (12) based on pulse pressure and the arterial volume change.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A device (10) for determining an arterial compliance of a subject (12), comprising: an inflatable cuff (14) which is attachable to a body part of the subject (12); inflation and deflation means (16) for inflating and deflating the cuff (14); a pressure sensor (18) which is configured to sense a pressure signal (52) that is indicative of a pressure within the cuff (14); a second sensor (20) which is at least partly integrated in the cuff (14) and configured to sense a second signal (56) that is responsive to expansions and contractions of the cuff (14) caused by a pulsating blood flow in an artery (50) of the subject (12); and a processing unit (22) which is configured: (i) to evaluate at least parts of the pressure signal (52) and the second signal (56) that are recorded while the cuff (14) is attached to the body part of the subject (12) and being deflated from above systolic pressure to below diastolic pressure, (ii) to determine based on said part of the pressure signal (52) a pulse pressure (ÎÎ¡) of the subject (12), (iii) to determine based on said part of the second signal (56) an arterial volume change (AVart) of the artery (50) of the subject (12) for each of a plurality of cardiac cycles recorded while the cuff (14) is being deflated from above systolic pressure to below diastolic pressure; and (iv) to determine the arterial compliance (C) of the subject (12) either by determining a maximum arterial volume change (AV ar t max) of the arterial volume changes (AV ar t) determined for each of the plurality of cardiac cycles and dividing the maximum arterial volume change (AVart max) by the pulse pressure (ÎÎ¡), or by dividing each of the determined arterial volume changes (AV ar t) of the plurality of cardiac cycles by the pulse pressure (ÎÎ¡) to receive a plurality of arterial compliance values, and then determining a maximum of the plurality of arterial compliance values. 2. The device according to claim 1, wherein the processing unit (22) is configured to determine the pulse pressure of the subject based on the pressure signal by means of an oscillometric evaluation. 3. The device according to claim 2, wherein the processing unit (22) is configured to determine the pulse pressure of the subject (12) not only based on the pressure signal, but also based on the second signal (56). 4. The device according to claim 1, wherein the second sensor (20) includes an optical sensor. 5. The device according to claim 4, wherein the optical sensor comprises an optical fibre (30) which is integrated into the cuff (14), a light source (32) which is configured to produce light that is coupled into the optical fibre (30) at a first end of the optical fibre (30), and a light detector (34) which is configured to detect the light at a second end of the optical fibre (30) opposite the first end. 6. The device according to claim 5, wherein the light detector (34) is configured to transform the detected light into an electrical signal that is proportional to an intensity of the detected light and that represents the second signal (56). 7. The device according to claim 5, wherein the cuff (14) comprises an inflatable part (38) and a stretchable carrier material (42) connected to the inflatable part (38), wherein the optical fibre (30) is integrated in the stretchable carrier material (42). 8. The device according to claim 7, wherein the stretchable carrier material (42) is arranged at a side of the cuff (14) that is configured to contact the skin of the subject (12). 9. The device according to claim 5, wherein the optical fibre (30) is arranged in the cuff as a folded winding. 10. The device according to claim 9, wherein the optical fibre (30) comprises at least two folded windings. 11. The device according to claim 1 , wherein the second sensor (20) includes a strain gauge. 12. A method for determining an arterial compliance of a subject (12), comprising the steps of: attaching an inflatable cuff (14) to a body part of the subject (12); inflating the cuff (14) to a first pressure above systolic pressure; deflating the cuff (14) to a second pressure below diastolic pressure; sensing a pressure signal (52) that is indicative of a pressure within the cuff (14) while the cuff (14) is being deflated from above systolic pressure to below diastolic pressure; sensing a second signal (56) that is responsive to expansions and contractions of the cuff (14) caused by a pulsating blood flow of the subject (12) while the cuff (14) is deflated from above systolic pressure to below diastolic pressure; - determining a pulse pressure (ÎÎ¡) of the subject (12), based on the pressure signal; determining, based on the second signal (56), an arterial volume change (AVart) of an artery (50) of the subject (12) for each of a plurality of cardiac cycles recorded while the cuff (14) is being deflated from above systolic pressure to below diastolic pressure; and determining the arterial compliance of the subject (12) either by determining a maximum arterial volume change (AV ar t max) of the arterial volume changes (AV ar t) determined for each of the plurality of cardiac cycles and dividing the maximum arterial volume change (AVart max) by the pulse pressure (ÎÎ¡), or by dividing each of the determined arterial volume changes (AV ar t) of the plurality of cardiac cycles by the pulse pressure (ÎÎ¡) to receive a plurality of arterial compliance values, and then determining a maximum of the plurality of arterial compliance values. 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein the pulse pressure of the subject (12) is determined by means of an osciUometric evaluation either based on the pressure signal (52) or based on the pressure signal (52) and the second signal (56).
